title: RevReel - Car Editing Reels Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Create homepage with hero banner and featured reel cards showcasing car editing content
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage renders a hero section with site branding and tagline
Featured reels section shows at least a grid of reel cards
Each reel card displays thumbnail image, title, uploader, and view count
CTA button navigates to the browse/gallery page

file_operations.description: Create homepage component with hero section (including site title and tagline), featured reels grid section fetching data via getFeaturedReels, and CTA button linking to browse page. Each reel card displays thumbnail, title, uploader, and view count. Wire the generated hero-banner.dim_1920x600.png image from frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1920x600.png as the hero background.
Create reusable reel card component displaying thumbnail image, title, uploader name, and view count with hover effects and click navigation to reel detail page.
Set up React Router routes for HomePage at root path ('/') and wire navigation structure for the application.

requirements.summary: Implement reel upload form allowing users to submit car editing reels with metadata
acceptanceCriteria: Upload/submit page renders a form with fields: title, description, video URL, thumbnail URL, and tags
Form validates required fields before submission
Submitted reels are persisted in the Motoko backend
User receives a success confirmation after submission

file_operations.description: Create upload page with a form containing input fields for title, description, videoUrl, thumbnailUrl, uploader, and tags (comma-separated or multi-select). Implement form validation ensuring required fields are filled. On submit, call the backend submitReel method with NewReel data. Display success confirmation message after successful submission and provide option to navigate to browse page or submit another reel.
Add route for UploadPage at '/upload' path and include navigation link in the app header or navigation bar.

requirements.summary: Build browse/gallery page with filtering, search, and responsive grid display of all reels
acceptanceCriteria: Browse page displays all reels in a responsive grid
Tag/category filter buttons narrow the displayed reels
Search input filters reels by title or description
Clicking a reel card navigates to the reel detail page

file_operations.description: Create browse/gallery page that fetches all reels via getAllReels backend method. Implement a responsive grid layout displaying ReelCard components. Add tag filter buttons that call filterByTag backend method when clicked. Include a search input field that filters reels client-side by title or description. Wire each reel card click to navigate to the reel detail page with the reel ID.
Add route for BrowsePage at '/browse' path and ensure navigation from homepage CTA and app navigation bar links to this page.

requirements.summary: Create reel detail page with embedded video player, metadata display, and view count increment
acceptanceCriteria: Reel detail page renders an embedded video player using the stored video URL
Full title, description, uploader name, tags, and view count are displayed
View count increments on each page visit via a backend call
Page includes a back navigation link to the browse gallery

file_operations.description: Create reel detail page that extracts reel ID from URL params and fetches reel data via getReel backend method. Display an embedded iframe video player using the videoUrl (supporting YouTube/Vimeo embeds). Show full title, description, uploader name, tags array, and view count. On component mount, call incrementViewCount backend method with the reel ID to increment view count. Include a back button or link navigating to '/browse'.
Add route for ReelDetailPage at '/reel/:id' path to enable dynamic reel detail navigation.

requirements.summary: Apply dark cinematic visual theme with black/dark-grey backgrounds, amber/orange accents, bold typography, and edge-lit card effects
acceptanceCriteria: Global color palette uses deep black/dark-grey backgrounds with amber/orange accents
Typography uses bold, modern sans-serif fonts
Cards have a subtle glowing border or edge-lit effect
Theme is applied consistently across all pages and components

file_operations.description: Update global styles to establish dark cinematic theme with deep black/dark-grey backgrounds (#0a0a0a, #1a1a1a), amber/orange accent colors (#ff8c00, #ffa500), bold white text (#ffffff), and modern sans-serif font stack (e.g., 'Inter', 'Roboto', sans-serif). Define base text and background colors for body and root elements.

Extend Tailwind theme with custom color palette including 'cinematic-black', 'cinematic-grey', 'amber-accent', and 'orange-accent'. Add custom box-shadow utilities for edge-lit glow effects on cards (e.g., glowing amber border). Configure font family for bold, modern sans-serif typography.
Apply edge-lit card styling using Tailwind custom shadow utilities (glowing amber border effect) and ensure dark background with white text and amber accents on hover.

requirements.summary: Use generated hero background image in homepage hero section
acceptanceCriteria: Homepage hero section renders the generated image as a full-width background
Image is loaded from frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1920x600.png

file_operations.description: Set the hero section background image to the generated asset at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1920x600.png using inline styles or Tailwind background utilities. Ensure the image covers the full width of the hero section with appropriate background positioning and sizing (e.g., background-size: cover, background-position: center).
